user_query:
I’m training for a marathon and trying to add consistent cross-training. Please build a small validator that reads my proposed cross-training plan and run log, applies my weekly targets and restrictions, and then produces three outputs under the workspace:

1) out/validation_report.json — a machine-readable report with weekly aggregates and pass/fail checks.
2) out/summary.md — a short human-readable summary of the findings.
3) out/email_to_coach.txt — a concise email draft to Coach Alex summarizing the results and any issues.

Inputs (provided in the workspace):
- input/plan.csv — my proposed cross-training sessions for two weeks.
- input/run_log.csv — my running workouts for the same period (used to check conflicts with tempo/interval/long runs).
- input/rules.yaml — weekly targets for cross-training volume/variety and rest days.
- input/notes.txt — additional Restriction: lines that must be enforced.

Requirements:
- Weeks should be grouped Monday–Sunday based on the dates in the files (e.g., 2024-05-06 to 2024-05-12, and 2024-05-13 to 2024-05-19 in these inputs).
- Compute per-week cross-training aggregates from input/plan.csv: number of cross-training days, total cross-training minutes, count of unique focuses, whether at least one flexibility session occurred (flexibility focuses are listed in rules.yaml), and whether there was at least one full rest day (a date with neither a run in input/run_log.csv nor a cross-training session in input/plan.csv).
- Enforce weekly targets from input/rules.yaml (min/max cross-training days and minutes, min unique focuses, require at least one flexibility session, require at least one rest day per week).
- Enforce all Restriction: lines in input/notes.txt. For this dataset, at minimum these two must be applied: (a) no lower-body strength within 1 day before or after a long run; (b) no high-intensity cross-training on the same day as tempo or interval runs. Only lines starting with "Restriction:" are mandatory; ignore "Preference:" lines for pass/fail.
- The JSON report at out/validation_report.json should include:
  - weeks: an array of objects with fields week_start (YYYY-MM-DD), week_end (YYYY-MM-DD), cross_training_days (int), total_cross_training_minutes (int), unique_focuses (int), has_flexibility_session (bool), has_rest_day (bool).
  - rule_checks: an object with a key for each checked rule (min_days, max_days, min_minutes, max_minutes, min_unique_focuses, require_flexibility_session, require_rest_day, notes_no_lb_strength_near_long, notes_no_high_intensity_on_tempo_or_interval). Each rule entry should have passed (bool) and a violations array listing dates and/or week ranges where the rule was violated.
  - aggregates: overall averages across all weeks: avg_cross_training_days (float) and avg_cross_training_minutes (float).
  - overall_status: "PASS" if and only if all required rules pass; otherwise "FAIL".
- The human summary at out/summary.md should briefly list the per-week stats and enumerate any violations with dates.
- The email draft at out/email_to_coach.txt should address "Coach Alex", summarize the weekly averages and any flagged issues, and propose at least one concrete adjustment idea for each type of violation found. Keep it polite and concise.
- Do not use any external resources. All computations must come from the provided inputs.

Notes to help you validate: the plan intentionally contains some conflicts (e.g., a high-intensity cycling session on a tempo day, and lower-body strength placed near long runs). Please ensure those are detected.

Deliverables:
- out/validation_report.json
- out/summary.md
- out/email_to_coach.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
